javaBlocks.report: Mobile app displays active alarm notification
javaBlocks.rawText:
REPORT("Mobile app displays active alarm notification");
        mobileApp.assertAlarmNotificationVisible();

javaBlocks.report: User dismisses alert from mobile app
javaBlocks.rawText:
REPORT("User dismisses alert from mobile app");
        mobileApp.dismissAlert(config.getMasterCode());

javaBlocks.report: Panel returns to ARMED STAY after dismissal
javaBlocks.rawText:
REPORT("Panel returns to ARMED STAY after dismissal");
        panel.assertState(PanelState.ARMED_STAY);

javaBlocks.report: Mobile app confirms alert is dismissed
javaBlocks.rawText:
REPORT("Mobile app confirms alert is dismissed");
        mobileApp.assertAlarmNotificationGone();

zephyrSteps.description: Trigger a door or window sensor to activate alarm
zephyrSteps.expectedResult: Panel transitions to ALARM state within 30 seconds
zephyrSteps.manualStep: Open door/window sensor while system is armed

zephyrSteps.description: Mobile app shows active alarm notification banner
zephyrSteps.expectedResult: Push notification delivered and in-app banner displayed within 10 seconds
zephyrSteps.manualStep: Check mobile app for alarm notification
zephyrSteps.automationStep: Assert alarm notification banner is visible in app
zephyrSteps.manualResult: Push notification and in-app banner visible

zephyrSteps.description: User dismisses alert by entering master code in app
zephyrSteps.expectedResult: Alert dismissed, panel enters grace period before returning to armed state
zephyrSteps.manualStep: Enter master code in dismiss alert dialog

zephyrSteps.description: Panel returns to prior armed state after alert is dismissed
zephyrSteps.expectedResult: Panel shows ARMED STAY status. No alarm sound.

zephyrSteps.description: Mobile app notification clears and shows normal armed status
zephyrSteps.expectedResult: Alarm banner dismissed. App shows green ARMED STAY indicator.
zephyrSteps.manualStep: Verify app returns to normal armed display
zephyrSteps.automationStep: Assert notification gone, assert armed indicator visible
zephyrSteps.manualResult: App shows normal ARMED STAY status

matches.issue: Automation asserts notification visible but does not verify the 10-second delivery window. Tests pass when notification arrives at 8s but could miss a timing regression at 12s.
Step asserts notification is gone but does not assert the ARMED STAY indicator is visible separately. A bug could hide the indicator while still passing the notification-gone check.

analysis.reasoning: The automation covers the notification delivery path but omits the 10-second SLA required by the Zephyr step. The assertion is structurally correct but under-constrained — it would pass on a system with a 30-second notification delay. Whether this constitutes a real coverage gap depends on whether timing SLAs are tracked separately in monitoring. Recommend adding a timeout assertion: assertAlarmNotificationVisible(maxSeconds=10).
analysis.recommendation: Add explicit timeout parameter to assertAlarmNotificationVisible() call

analysis.reasoning: Step 5 requires two distinct assertions: (1) alarm banner is gone, and (2) the normal armed state indicator is visible. The automation only checks one. This could produce a false positive if the app crashes or hangs in a blank state after dismissal. The risk is low in practice because other assertions would likely catch a full crash, but it represents a gap in fine-grained UI state verification.
